projects.name: TraceTree
projects.short_description: Runtime behavioral analysis tool for detecting software supply chain attacks.
projects.description: TraceTree monitors execution paths and dependencies to identify suspicious behavior and improve visibility into software supply chains.

projects.name: Switchboard
projects.short_description: Intelligent MCP routing server for AI coding agents.
projects.description: Switchboard dynamically routes requests across models and providers to optimize cost, latency, and performance.

projects.name: Tabib (طبيب)
projects.description: Tabib uses local AI models to provide medical guidance while preserving user privacy and reducing dependence on centralized infrastructure.

projects.name: EvidenceChain
projects.description: EvidenceChain assists digital investigations with self-correction capabilities and support for SANS SIFT workflows.

form.question: How to support
form.response: Star the repositories, report bugs, open issues, suggest improvements, or contribute code and documentation.

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: I build tools that improve transparency, privacy, and security.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: Ship imperfect things. Waiting for perfection means nobody gets to use or improve your work.

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
What's your open-source villain origin story?
form.response: I wrote a research paper on API vulnerabilities and needed tools that didn't exist, so I built them myself.

form.question: If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
